prompt: Who are the top 10 highest rated professors based on student evaluations?

inputData.question: Who are the top 10 highest rated professors based on student evaluations?

response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"success","steps":{"input":{"question":"Who are the top 10 highest rated professors based on student evaluations?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"Who are the top 10 highest rated professors based on student evaluations?"},"startedAt":1771855574313,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"The question asks for the top 10 highest rated professors based on student evaluations. I need to join professors with course_sections, enrollments, and course_evaluations to get the professor_rating from evaluations. I'll aggregate by professor and calculate the average professor_rating, then order by that rating descending and limit to 10.","sql":"
SELECT 
  p.id,
  p.first_name,
  p.last_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric, 2) AS average_professor_rating,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS total_evaluations
FROM professors p
INNER JOIN course_sections cs ON p.id = cs.professor_id
INNER JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
INNER JOIN course_evaluations ce ON e.id = ce.enrollment_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY average_professor_rating DESC, total_evaluations DESC
LIMIT 10
","assumptions":["Assuming 'professor_rating' column in course_evaluations represents the student evaluation rating for the professor","Assuming we should calculate average professor_rating across all evaluations for each professor","Assuming we should include professors with at least one evaluation","Assuming ties are broken by total number of evaluations (secondary sort)"]},"endedAt":1771855616587},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"

LIMIT 10

visualization.title: Top 10 Highest Rated Professors by Student Evaluations

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT 
  p.id,
  p.first_name,
  p.last_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric, 2) AS average_professor_rating,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS total_evaluations
FROM professors p
INNER JOIN course_sections cs ON p.id = cs.professor_id
INNER JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
INNER JOIN course_evaluations ce ON e.id = ce.enrollment_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY average_professor_rating DESC, total_evaluations DESC
LIMIT 10

visualization.title: Top 10 Highest Rated Professors by Student Evaluations

result.sql:
SELECT 
  p.id,
  p.first_name,
  p.last_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric, 2) AS average_professor_rating,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS total_evaluations
FROM professors p
INNER JOIN course_sections cs ON p.id = cs.professor_id
INNER JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
INNER JOIN course_evaluations ce ON e.id = ce.enrollment_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY average_professor_rating DESC, total_evaluations DESC
LIMIT 10
